content.problemStatement:
You're given an array AAA of NNN integers. You need to find the minimum cost of creating another array BBB of NNN integers with the following properties


Bi‚Č•0B_i \ge 0Bi‚Äč‚Č•0 for each 1‚Č§i‚Č§N1 \leq i \leq N1‚Č§i‚Č§N
The GCD of adjacent elements of BBB is equal to 111, i.e, gcd‚Ā°(Bi,Bi+1)=1\gcd(B_i, B_{i+1}) = 1gcd(Bi‚Äč,Bi+1‚Äč)=1 for each 1‚Č§i<N1 \leq i \lt N1‚Č§i<N
The cost of creating BBB is defined as follows:

‚ąĎi=1N2‚ą£Ai‚ąíBi‚ą£\sum_{i=1}^{N} 2^{|A_i - B_i |}i=1‚ąĎN‚Äč2‚ą£Ai‚Äč‚ąíBi‚Äč‚ą£Find the **minimum** possible cost to create the array BBB. Since the answer can be huge print it modulo 109+710^9+7109+7

**Note:** You need to minimize the value of total cost of creating the array BBB, and then print this minimum value modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7. For example, suppose there is a way to create the required BBB with a cost of 500500500, and another way to do it with a cost of 109+710^9 + 7109+7 (which is 0(mod109+7)0 \pmod {10^9 + 7}0(mod109+7)). The output in this case would be 500500500 and not 000.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN - the length of the array AAA
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1,A_2,\ldots,A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum cost of creating the array BBB, modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 10410^4104.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The given input already satisfies all the conditions, hence we can take B=AB = AB=A with a cost of 20+20+20=32^0 + 2^0 + 2^0 = 320+20+20=3.
Test case 222: One optimal way is to choose BBB as [5,11][ 5, 11 ][5,11], with a cost of 20+21=32^0 + 2^1 = 320+21=3.
